title: dryopteris percivalii
intro: Dryopteris percivalii, also known as Percival's wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to the UK. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Dryopteris percivalii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and support the immune system. Dryopteris percivalii is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications such as salves and creams.
